title: Air-fryer roast potatoes
subtitle: Crisp-shelled roasties without heating the oven
excerpt: Potatoes parboiled until fluffy, shaken to rough up the edges, tossed in oil, and roasted in the air fryer at 200°C for 25 to 30 minutes. The parboil is not optional: air-fried potatoes without it produce a crisped exterior and a waxy, underdone interior.
sourceNotes: Roast potatoes are one of the most important dishes in British cooking, central to the Sunday roast. The traditional method uses a very hot oven with a large amount of fat (goose fat, duck fat, or lard). The air fryer produces a comparable result with less fat and faster than the oven, though it cannot cook the same volume in one batch.

content.text: The parboil is not optional. The air fryer cannot cook a dense 5 cm potato all the way through in the time it takes to crisp the outside. Parboiling for 8 minutes cooks the interior and creates a rough, starchy surface. That rough surface is what the air fryer crisps into the craggy exterior that makes a good roast potato.

items.name: Potato
items.prepNote: Maris Piper or King Edward, peeled and cut into large pieces

items.name: Vegetable oil
items.prepNote: or goose fat for a richer result

content.text: Bring a large pan of salted water to the boil. Add the {{potato}} pieces and boil for 8 minutes until the edges look slightly fluffy and a knife meets a little resistance at the centre. Drain thoroughly and return to the pot. Replace the lid and shake the pot vigorously several times to roughen all the surfaces. Spread the potatoes on a tray for 2 minutes to steam dry. Toss with {{vegetable-oil}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{rosemary}} if using. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Arrange the potatoes in the basket in a single layer. Cook at 200°C for 25 to 30 minutes, turning twice, until deep golden and crisp on all sides.

items.fix: The parboil should go until the edges are visibly fluffy and breaking slightly. Cut pieces no larger than 5 cm. Very large pieces can be halved before tossing in oil
items.cause: Parboil time too short, or potatoes cut too large
items.symptom: Potatoes crisped outside but still waxy inside

items.fix: The shaking step is what creates the fluffy surface the fat clings to. Let the potatoes steam dry on the tray before oiling; wet potatoes produce a soft outside, not a crisp one
items.cause: Roughing step skipped, or potatoes too wet when tossed in oil
items.symptom: No craggy crust forming despite correct temperature

items.fix: Do not attempt to turn the potatoes until they release easily from the basket. If they are sticking, give them another 2 minutes. A properly crisped surface will lift cleanly
items.cause: Turning too early before the crust has set
items.symptom: Potatoes sticking to the basket and tearing when turned

content.text: Air-fryer roast potatoes are a weeknight replacement for the oven version when you do not want to heat a full oven for a side dish. They are slightly less grand than oven roasties cooked in a large tray of fat, but for two to four people they are faster, produce less washing up, and are consistently crisp.
